mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal aggressive monetary accommodation. This compression of the risk-free rate reduces equity discount rates, increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.16% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with policy rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or forward returns in triggered periods show no statistically significant outperformance (t-test p > 0.10) versus non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 7.5% indicates slack labor market conditions that constrain wage growth and corporate cost pressures. This reduces earnings headwinds and supports equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns as the market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or weak labor market expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during high-unemployment periods (UNRATE > 7.5%) do not exceed returns during tight-labor periods by at least 150 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=708k, RSAFS=$404.6B indicate moderate demand
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 when both HOUST and RSAFS grow simultaneously, or joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 97.0 signal supply-side weakness that reduces competitive pressures on corporate margins. Lower supply growth relative to demand supports pricing power and equity returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices margin expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when INDPRO < 97.0, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between low-INDPRO and high-INDPRO regimes (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50k month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 7.5%. This combination signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative 20-day forward returns as investors reduce growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS=133.088M, UNRATE=8.2% indicate weak labor dynamics
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 when both conditions are met, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 230.0 while federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, indicating real rates are deeply negative. This monetary accommodation in an inflationary environment compresses real discount rates and supports equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 when both conditions hold, or 60-day forward returns in this regime underperform baseline by more than 100 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates quarter-over-quarter below 0.5% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that prompts Federal Reserve to cut rates. Anticipation of monetary easing produces positive 20-day equity returns as discount rates compress ahead of policy action.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1=$13,491.4B indicates moderate growth trajectory
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with policy accommodation expected
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 when GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5% annualized, or forward returns in slowdown periods do not significantly exceed returns in expansion periods (t-test p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized supply and labor contraction. This dual weakness indicates recession risk that triggers equity sell-offs and negative 5-day forward returns as investors reduce risk exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND PAYEMS monthly change < 0
regime_hint: recession onset or severe economic contraction
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 when both conditions are met, or negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances (binomial test p > 0.08).
